text:
Once upon a time, there was a frog who loved singing.

Every day after dinner, he would go out in his tiny little froggy wardrobe and practice his opera. He croaked away on scale-back guitars and baritone opera horns.

One day, he saw a butterfly fluttering. She was so pretty, with big, beautiful eyes. The frog croaked her lilting melody.

She chatted with the frog. "Oh, I love opera. I'm a professional singer."

The frog crooned something back. She swooned. "I have those big wings. You could do operas there."

The little butterfly landed on a leaf. "Maybe sometime?"

The frog croaked. "Yeah?"

She fluttered up onto a butterfly feeder hanging outside. "Can't."

She crooned a pretty song. The frog croaked along.

Hours passed. The sun went down. She danced and sang.

Suddenly the ground shook. A big, terrible turtle stomped out.
